content:
# 🚨 MANDATORY: Team Memory System 🚨

A shared team memory stores critical gotchas, conventions, and debugging insights across sessions.

## REQUIRED: /recall BEFORE Starting Any Task

You MUST run /recall at the start of every task to check for relevant prior knowledge. Skipping this risks repeating known mistakes and wasting time on already-solved problems.

```
/recall "<topic or keyword relevant to your task>"
```

Examples:
- Working on Stripe integration? → /recall "Stripe"
- Fixing a database query? → /recall "database" or /recall "Drizzle"
- Debugging a test? → /recall "testing patterns"
- Working on team creation? → /recall "team" or /recall "createId"
- Touching waivers? → /recall "waiver"

**Do NOT skip this step.** Past sessions have stored critical gotchas that will save significant debugging time.

## REQUIRED: /remember After Discoveries

When you discover something useful — a gotcha, root cause, convention, or debugging insight — you MUST save it immediately with /remember. Do not wait until the end of the session.

```
/remember "<what you learned>" --category=gotcha|convention|debugging|architecture|workflow --priority=critical|moderate|ephemeral
```

## Summary

- **BEFORE work**: /recall to load relevant memories (MANDATORY)
- **DURING work**: /remember as soon as you discover something worth saving (MANDATORY)
- Failure to use team memory means the team loses knowledge and repeats mistakes.

## deepwiki
DeepWiki MCP provides AI-powered documentation for GitHub repositories.

Available tools:
- read_wiki_structure: Get a list of documentation topics for a repository
- read_wiki_contents: View full documentation about a repository
- ask_question: Ask any question about a repository and get an AI-powered answer
- list_available_repos: List your available repositories (private mode only)
- generate_wiki: Generate a codebase wiki for a repository — only use when explicitly requested by the user (private mode only)
- devin_knowledge_manage: Manage Devin knowledge notes and suggestions — list, search, get, create, update, delete notes, view folder structure, list/view/dismiss knowledge suggestions (private mode only)
- devin_playbook_manage: Manage Devin playbooks — list, get, create, update, delete (private mode only)
- devin_schedule_manage: Manage scheduled Devin sessions — list, get, create, update, delete (private mode only)
- devin_session_create: Create one or more child Devin sessions (private mode only)
- devin_session_interact: Manage a Devin session — get status, send messages, sleep/terminate/archive, read messages & attachments, manage tags (private mode only)
- devin_session_events: Inspect session events — list summaries, fetch full details, or search event contents (private mode only)
- devin_session_search: Search and filter Devin sessions (private mode only)
- list_integrations: List all native integrations and MCP servers with their status and settings URLs (private mode only)

## plugin:linear:linear
When passing string values to tools, send the content directly without escape sequences. For example, use real newlines in markdown content rather than literal backslash-n (\n) characters.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- agent-browser: Browser automation CLI for AI agents. Use when the user needs to interact with websites, including navigating pages, filling forms, clicking buttons, taking screenshots, extracting data, testing web apps, or automating any browser task. Triggers include requests to "open a website", "fill out a form", "click a button", "take a screenshot", "scrape data from a page", "test this web app", "login to a site", "automate browser actions", or any task requiring programmatic web interaction.
- seo-aeo-best-practices: SEO and AEO best practices for metadata, Open Graph, sitemaps, robots.txt, hreflang, JSON-LD structured data, EEAT, and content optimized for search engines and AI answer surfaces. Use this skill when implementing page SEO, technical SEO, schema markup, international SEO, AI-overview readiness, or improving content for Google, ChatGPT, Perplexity, and similar assistants.
- self-improving-agent: A universal self-improving agent that learns from ALL skill experiences. Uses multi-memory architecture (semantic + episodic + working) to continuously evolve the codebase. Auto-triggers on skill completion/error with hooks-based self-correction.
- web-design-guidelines: Review UI code for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Use when asked to "review my UI", "check accessibility", "audit design", "review UX", or "check my site against best practices".
- linear: Manage issues, projects & team workflows in Linear. Use when the user wants to read, create or updates tickets in Linear.
- breadboarding: Transform a workflow description into affordance tables showing UI and Code affordances with their wiring. Use to map existing systems or design new ones from shaped parts.
- repo-autopsy: Clone a GitHub repo into a local cache and perform deep analysis using full local toolchain (rg, ast-grep, git, fd, tokei, gitleaks). Use when the user wants to inspect, explore, audit, or understand an external GitHub repository — structure, dependencies, hotspots, exports, secrets, code stats, git blame, or targeted file reads. Triggers on phrases like "autopsy", "analyze this repo", "clone and explore", "what's inside owner/repo", or any request to investigate a third-party GitHub project without adding it to the current working tree.
- adr-skill: Create and maintain Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) optimized for agentic coding workflows. Use when you need to propose, write, update, accept/reject, deprecate, or supersede an ADR; bootstrap an adr folder and index; consult existing ADRs before implementing changes; or enforce ADR conventions. This skill uses Socratic questioning to capture intent before drafting, and validates output against an agent-readiness checklist.
- breadboard-reflection: Breadboard Analysis
- stripe-best-practices: Best practices for building Stripe integrations
- frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- vercel-react-best-practices: React and Next.js performance optimization guidelines from Vercel Engineering. This skill should be used when writing, reviewing, or refactoring React/Next.js code to ensure optimal performance patterns. Triggers on tasks involving React components, Next.js pages, data fetching, bundle optimization, or performance improvements.
- vercel-composition-patterns: React composition patterns that scale. Use when refactoring components with boolean prop proliferation, building flexible component libraries, or designing reusable APIs. Triggers on tasks involving compound components, render props, context providers, or component architecture. Includes React 19 API changes.
- shaping: Use this methodology when collaboratively shaping a solution with the user - iterating on problem definition (requirements) and solution options (shapes).
- tanstack-start-server-only: TanStack Start server-only patterns using createServerOnlyFn and cloudflare:workers env access.
Use when: accessing Cloudflare environment variables (APP_URL, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, etc.),
creating server-only utility functions, working with Stripe or other server-only APIs,
or when you need to ensure code only runs on the server and throws if called from client.
Covers createServerOnlyFn vs createServerFn differences, env access patterns, and centralized
server-only utilities.
- documentation: Centralizes Diataxis documentation framework guidance so Claude can write, review, or improve documentation following the four distinct types (tutorials, how-to guides, reference, explanation).
- athlete-docs: Documentation guidance for competition athletes and volunteers in WODsmith. Use when writing, reviewing, or improving athlete-facing documentation including registration, scheduling, workout viewing, leaderboards, check-in, and volunteer coordination.
- ralph-mode: Autonomous AI coding loop that ships features while you sleep. Use when the user wants to run Ralph, start an autonomous coding session, work overnight, or let Claude implement multiple user stories autonomously. Triggers on "ralph", "autonomous mode", "overnight coding", "autonomous loop", or requests to implement multiple stories without supervision.
- server-fn-auth: Authentication and authorization patterns for TanStack Start server functions.
Use when: adding auth to a new server function, fixing missing authorization,
checking user permissions, protecting endpoints, or implementing organizer/volunteer
access control for competitions.
- test: Primary entry point for testing guidance. Routes to appropriate test type (unit, integration, E2E) based on what you're testing. Use when asked about "test", "testing", "write tests", "TDD", or test strategy. Embeds Testing Trophy philosophy and TDD Red-Green-Refactor as core discipline.
- claim-url-lookup: Look up claim URLs for placeholder users created via manual registration. Use when needing to find the sign-up claim link for a specific email address, debug claim token issues, or verify claim tokens exist in local KV. Triggers on "claim URL", "claim token", "claim link", "placeholder user sign-up link", or requests to find the registration invite link for an email.
- competition-organizer-docs: Generate and review documentation for competition organizers using Diataxis framework. Use when writing docs for organizer workflows like creating competitions, managing divisions, scheduling heats, handling registrations, entering results, or managing volunteers/judges.
- type-safety: Fix type assertions and improve TypeScript type safety. Use when encountering 'as unknown as' casts, manual type definitions that duplicate schema types, or unclear type errors in database queries, especially with Drizzle ORM relations. Also use when verifying types
- date-timezone: Handle date and timezone conversions correctly in forms and displays. Use when working with: Date inputs, new Date(), formatDate, toLocaleDateString, getDate(), getMonth(), getFullYear(), date parsing, UTC conversion, timezone issues, dates displaying wrong day, date-only fields in forms, YYYY-MM-DD parsing. Triggers: dates off by one day, timezone bugs, form date handling, competition dates, event dates, calendar dates.
- integration-test: Guide for writing integration tests with Vitest and Testing Library. Use when testing multi-component workflows, database interactions, React components with context providers, or full user flows. Covers the Testing Trophy philosophy (integration > unit), factory patterns for test data, MSW for network mocking, async testing patterns (waitFor, findBy), and custom render with providers. Use this for tests that cross multiple modules or layers of the application.
- permissions: Guide for working with team-based permissions and authorization in the WODsmith codebase. Use when touching TEAM_PERMISSIONS constants, hasTeamPermission/requireTeamPermission functions, adding permission checks to actions or server functions, creating features requiring authorization, or ensuring client-server permission consistency.
- unit-test: Guide for writing unit tests with Vitest. Use when writing tests for service functions, pure logic, or webhook handlers. Covers TDD Red-Green-Refactor cycle, Arrange-Act-Assert pattern, anti-patterns, pure function testing, and mocking at boundaries.
- tanstack-start-boundaries: TanStack Start server/client code boundary patterns. Use when: debugging virtual module resolution errors (tanstack-start-injected-head-scripts, tanstack-start-manifest, #tanstack-router-entry), importing from @tanstack/react-start/server, using getCookie/getRequestHeaders/getRequestUrl, adding providers to router.tsx, or when Vite fails to resolve TanStack Start imports. Covers createServerFn patterns, dynamic imports, and proper placement of server-only code.
- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills using TypeScript/Bun scripts (Windows-compatible). Use when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Claude's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations.
- drag-and-drop: Implement drag and drop using @atlaskit/pragmatic-drag-and-drop. Use when implementing sortable lists, reorderable items, kanban boards, or any drag-drop interactions. Covers draggable setup, drop targets, edge detection, drag previews, and critical state management patterns to avoid performance issues.
- my-pr-comments: Loads the pr review comments for the current branch.
- review: Centralizes persona-driven code reviews (Carmack, React core, Anders, React Router, Next.js, etc.) so Claude can pick or combine expert viewpoints when the user asks for a code review or perspective-specific critique. Use when user asks for code review, architecture feedback, or expert perspective on implementation.
- local-db: Handle local database schema changes with Drizzle and PlanetScale. Use when making schema changes to src/db/schema.ts, adding/modifying database tables or columns, or when asked about database migrations. Covers the push-based local development workflow and when to generate migrations.
- e2e-test: End-to-end testing with Playwright. Use when writing E2E tests, implementing page objects, setting up test fixtures, handling authentication in tests, adding visual regression testing, or configuring E2E tests for CI. Covers when to use E2E vs integration tests, critical path identification, page object pattern, test isolation, and CI considerations.
- team-memory: Store, search, and manage team memory from AI coding sessions. Use /remember to save observations (conventions, gotchas, debugging tips, architecture decisions, workflows). Use /recall to search memories by semantic query. Use /feedback to rate memory quality (helpful/harmful/irrelevant). Triggers on any request to save, recall, search, or rate team knowledge.
- alchemy-cloudflare: Alchemy IaC patterns for deploying TanStack Start apps to Cloudflare Workers with PlanetScale databases via Hyperdrive. Use when setting up new TanStack Start projects, configuring Alchemy deployments, working with PlanetScale/Drizzle migrations, local development with Cloudflare bindings, or deploying to custom domains.
- logging: Observability logging patterns for server functions in TanStack Start on Cloudflare Workers. Use when: adding logging to server functions, debugging request flows, tracking entity CRUD operations, correlating logs across requests, adding observability to new features, troubleshooting request tracing, working with PostHog/OpenTelemetry logs. Triggers: logInfo, logError, logWarning, withRequestContext, addRequestContextAttribute, logEntityCreated, request tracing, observability, correlation IDs.
- create-pr: Create a PR from current changes with PRD gif
- sync-diagrams: Store and synchronize digrams with codebase in ai/diagrams directory
- linear-project: Work on a Linear project with structured workflow
- test-pr: Review PR changes and provide comprehensive test coverage recommendations using unit, integration, and e2e testing skills
- fix-lint: /fix-lint
- swarm:inbox: Check swarm mail inbox for messages from other agents
- swarm:hive: Query and manage swarm tasks (cells)
- swarm:status: Check swarm coordination status - workers, messages, reservations
- swarm:swarm: Decompose a task into parallel subtasks and coordinate execution
- swarm:handoff: Properly end a swarm session - release reservations, sync state, generate continuation prompt
- swarm:swarm-coordination: Multi-agent coordination patterns for OpenCode swarm workflows. Use when work benefits from parallelization or coordination.
- swarm:always-on-guidance: Always-on rule-oriented guidance for claude-plugin agents. Use to align behavior, tool usage, and model-specific defaults (GPT-5.2-code vs Opus 4.5) while avoiding deprecated bd/cass references.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Before starting work, run `lat search` with one or more queries describing the user's intent.
ALWAYS do this, even when the task seems straightforward — search results may reveal critical design details, protocols, or constraints.
Use `lat section` to read the full content of relevant matches.
Do not read files, write code, or run commands until you have searched.

Remember: `lat.md/` must stay in sync with the codebase. If you change code, update the relevant sections in `lat.md/` and run `lat check` before finishing.

Base directory for this skill: /Users/zacjones/Documents/02.Areas/wodsmith/thewodapp-2/.claude/skills/team-memory

# Team Memory

Manage a shared team memory system backed by a Cloudflare Worker with semantic search.

## Commands

### /remember — Store an observation

Save a new observation to team memory.

```bash
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/remember.ts "<observation text>" [--category=<category>] [--priority=<priority>]
```

**Categories:** `convention`, `gotcha`, `debugging`, `architecture`, `workflow`
**Priorities:** `critical`, `moderate`, `ephemeral`

Defaults: category=convention, priority=moderate

Examples:
```bash
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/remember.ts "PlanetScale returns strings for COUNT columns — always wrap with Number()" --category=gotcha --priority=critical
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/remember.ts "Use ULID for ID generation, not CUID2" --category=convention
```

### /recall — Search memories

Search team memory by semantic query.

```bash
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/recall.ts "<query>" [--limit=<n>] [--category=<category>] [--priority=<priority>]
```

Default limit: 5. Results are ranked by relevance.

Examples:
```bash
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/recall.ts "database migration patterns"
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/recall.ts "Stripe" --category=gotcha --limit=10
```

### /feedback — Rate a memory

Provide feedback on a memory's usefulness.

```bash
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/feedback.ts <observation-id> <signal> [--note="<reason>"]
```

**Signals:** `helpful`, `harmful`, `irrelevant`

Examples:
```bash
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/feedback.ts 01J5K3M2N7 helpful --note="Saved me from a production bug"
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/feedback.ts 01J5K3M2N7 irrelevant
```

## Environment

Production URL: `https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev`

All commands below include the env var inline. If `TEAM_MEMORY_URL` is already set in your shell, the prefix is optional.

## Hooks

- **SessionStart**: Reminds the agent about the memory system and how to use /recall for task-relevant lookups
- **SessionEnd**: Reads transcript JSONL, extracts user/assistant messages, POSTs to `/sessions`
- **Export**: Syncs memories to MEMORY.md between `<!-- BEGIN TEAM-MEMORY -->` / `<!-- END TEAM-MEMORY -->` markers

[[lat.md/domain#Domain Model#Video Submissions#Score Adjustments]] (lat.md/domain.md:235-244)

> ### Score Adjustments
>
> Reviewers can adjust a submitted score with or without assigning a penalty. Penalty classification is optional on adjust; the score change is the primary action.
>
> The Verification Controls card surfaces three top-level actions: Verify, Adjust Score, and Mark Invalid. The Adjust Score form exposes a `Penalty (optional)` radio with None / Minor / Major, defaulting to None. When None is selected the client omits `penaltyType` from [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/submission-verification-fns.ts#verifySubmissionScoreFn]], which stores the adjustment with `verificationStatus = "adjusted"`, null penalty fields on `scoresTable`, and sets the video `reviewStatus` to `"adjusted"` rather than `"penalized"`. Minor/Major selections keep the original penalty flow with the denormalized `penaltyType`/`penaltyPercentage` columns populated. Both the organizer surface [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/events/$eventId/submissions/$submissionId.tsx]] and the volunteer surface [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/$slug/review/$eventId/$submissionId.tsx]] share this form.
>
> The adjust inputs mirror the athlete submission form: `parseScore` validates entries and shows a "Parsed as" preview, cap status is derived automatically from parsed time vs `event.workout.timeCap` (no manual Status picker), the reps-at-cap secondary input appears only when the cap is auto-hit, and a tiebreak input renders when `event.workout.tiebreakScheme` is set (POSTed as `tieBreakScore`). Shared input-hint helpers live in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/compete/score-entry-helpers.ts]] so `enter-score-form.tsx` and both review surfaces stay consistent. The "Athlete claimed" readout also appends the decoded tiebreak (`· TB: <value>`) whenever the workout defines a `tiebreakScheme`; when the athlete didn't enter one, the value renders as `—` so reviewers can tell at a glance that the tiebreak was skipped rather than assume none was required.
>
> The Adjust Score form's penalty-tiebreak state is seeded from `submission.score.tiebreakValue` (the decoded display string) rather than the empty string, so editing other fields without touching the tiebreak input preserves the existing value. Without that seed, the server would receive `tieBreakScore: undefined`, fail the `if (data.tieBreakScore && score.tiebreakScheme)` truthiness check, and persist `tiebreakValue: null`, silently erasing a previously-entered tiebreak.
>

## Team Memory Results (2)

- **[01KPC6GJ04ESBVQA6EM4SAAFAA]** (gotcha/critical, score: 0.96, candidate)
  Team video submissions in submitVideoFn only send the score with the FIRST slot (videoIndex=0) — the captain's first slot carries the team's score, subsequent slots intentionally arrive without one. Any server-side score-required validation in submitVideoFn must guard with 'data.videoIndex === 0' or it will reject teammate slots.

- **[01KJTYSWMA1AYJMPT3F9MCP90E]** (architecture/critical, score: 0.10, deprecated)
  Multi-tenant write endpoints: always validate the target record belongs to the actor's competition/org, not just that the record ID exists. scoreId alone is insufficient — verify competitionId too.

content.content:
## Team Memory Results (3)

- **[01KPMMGXTSW1HYWBTHPRXHBBBW]** (gotcha/critical, score: 1.00, candidate)
  When scoping score queries by scalingLevelId, treat null as its own scope (isNull(scoresTable.scalingLevelId)) — never drop the predicate. MySQL/PlanetScale UNIQUE indexes allow multiple NULLs, so a missing predicate falls back to (event, user) and leaks scores across divisions on shared workouts. Pattern recurs across athlete-score-fns, competition-score-fns, submission-verification-fns, video-submission-fns, organizer-athlete-fns, registration-fns, and the judge route. Also: scores.competition_event_id is misleadingly named — it stores a track_workouts.id, not a competition_events.id. Score deletes keyed off competitionEventsTable.id silently match nothing — use competitionEventsTable.trackWorkoutId instead.

- **[01KJENMM5FTAQ9JFNDPT8Z9B3H]** (architecture/critical, score: 0.08, deprecated)
  Series-level revenue aggregation: use single SQL query GROUP BY competitionId, divisionId instead of per-competition fetches — 3 queries total regardless of series size

- **[01KJREPP10EFSR23Z3ZWMTJ03M]** (gotcha/moderate, score: 0.09, deprecated)
  When building a majority-vote count Map from DB results with no ORDER BY, insertion order is non-deterministic and controls tie-breaking. Fix: add explicit tiebreaker (e.g. lexicographically smaller ID) after counting.

1	/**
2	 * Scoring Algorithm Factory
3	 *
4	 * Central factory that dispatches to the correct scoring algorithm based on
5	 * configuration. Provides a unified interface for calculating event points
6	 * regardless of which algorithm is being used.
7	 *
8	 * Algorithms:
9	 * - traditional: Fixed step point deduction from first place
10	 * - p_score: Performance-based scoring that rewards margin of victory
11	 * - custom: Template-based with optional place-specific overrides
12	 *
13	 * @see docs/plans/configurable-scoring-system.md
14	 */
15	
16	import type { WorkoutScheme } from "@/lib/scoring/types"
17	import type {
18	  PScoreConfig,
19	  ScoringConfig,
20	  TraditionalConfig,
21	} from "@/types/scoring"
22	import { calculateCustomPoints } from "./custom"
23	import { calculateOnlinePoints } from "./online"
24	import { calculatePScore, type PScoreInput } from "./p-score"
25	import { calculateTraditionalPoints } from "./traditional"
26	
27	export {
28	  calculateCustomPoints,
29	  generatePointsTable,
30	  WINNER_TAKES_MORE_TABLE,
31	} from "./custom"
32	export { calculateOnlinePoints } from "./online"
33	// Re-export algorithm implementations
34	export { calculatePScore, type PScoreInput, type PScoreResult } from "./p-score"
35	export { calculateTraditionalPoints } from "./traditional"
36	
37	/**
38	 * Input for event score calculation
39	 */
40	export interface EventScoreInput {
41	  userId: string
42	  /** Normalized score value (already encoded) */
43	  value: number
44	  /** Score status */
45	  status: "scored" | "cap" | "dnf" | "dns" | "withdrawn"
46	  /**
47	   * Pre-computed sort key for proper ordering.
48	   * Encodes status + value + secondaryValue + tiebreak into a single string.
49	   * If not provided, sorting falls back to value-only comparison.
50	   */
51	  sortKey?: string | null
52	}
53	
54	/**
55	 * Result of event score calculation
56	 */
57	export interface EventPointsResult {
58	  userId: string
59	  /** Points awarded for this event */
60	  points: number
61	  /** Rank in the event (1-indexed) */
62	  rank: number
63	}
64	
65	/**
66	 * Default scoring configuration
67	 */
68	export const DEFAULT_SCORING_CONFIG: ScoringConfig = {
69	  algorithm: "traditional",
70	  traditional: {
71	    step: 5,
72	    firstPlacePoints: 100,
73	  },
74	  tiebreaker: {
75	    primary: "countback",
76	  },
77	  statusHandling: {
78	    dnf: "last_place",
79	    dns: "zero",
80	    withdrawn: "exclude",
81	  },
82	}
83	
84	/**
85	 * Default traditional config
86	 */
87	export const DEFAULT_TRADITIONAL_CONFIG: TraditionalConfig = {
88	  step: 5,
89	  firstPlacePoints: 100,
90	}
91	
92	/**
93	 * Default P-Score config
94	 */
95	export const DEFAULT_PSCORE_CONFIG: PScoreConfig = {
96	  allowNegatives: true,
97	  medianField: "top_half",
98	}
99	
100	/**
101	 * Schemes where lower values are better (ascending sort)
102	 */
103	const ASCENDING_SCHEMES = new Set<WorkoutScheme>(["time", "time-with-cap"])
104	
105	/**
106	 * Sort scores by performance, using sortKey when available for proper
107	 * secondary value (reps at cap) and tiebreak ordering.
108	 */
109	function sortScoresByPerformance(
110	  scores: EventScoreInput[],
111	  scheme: WorkoutScheme,
112	): EventScoreInput[] {
113	  const isAscending = ASCENDING_SCHEMES.has(scheme)
114	
115	  return [...scores].sort((a, b) => {
116	    // If both have sortKey, use it for complete ordering
117	    if (a.sortKey && b.sortKey) {
118	      // sortKey is zero-padded string, lexicographic comparison works
119	      return a.sortKey.localeCompare(b.sortKey)
120	    }
121	
122	    // Fallback: status-based sorting for time-with-cap
123	    if (scheme === "time-with-cap") {
124	      if (a.status === "cap" && b.status !== "cap") return 1
125	      if (a.status !== "cap" && b.status === "cap") return -1
126	    }
127	
128	    // Fallback: value-only comparison
129	    return isAscending ? a.value - b.value : b.value - a.value
130	  })
131	}
132	
133	/**
134	 * Calculate event points using the configured scoring algorithm.
135	 *
136	 * This is the main entry point for scoring calculations. It dispatches
137	 * to the appropriate algorithm based on configuration.
138	 *
139	 * @param eventId - Unique identifier for the event (used for caching)
140	 * @param scores - Array of athlete scores for this event
141	 * @param scheme - Workout scheme (determines sort direction)
142	 * @param config - Scoring configuration
143	 * @returns Map of userId → points for this event
144	 *
145	 * @example
146	 * ```ts
147	 * const pointsMap = calculateEventPoints(
148	 *   "event-123",
149	 *   [
150	 *     { userId: "a", value: 60000, status: "scored" },
151	 *     { userId: "b", value: 65000, status: "scored" },
152	 *   ],
153	 *   "time",
154	 *   { algorithm: "traditional", traditional: { step: 5, firstPlacePoints: 100 } }
155	 * )
156	 * // → Map { "a" => 100, "b" => 95 }
157	 * ```
158	 */
159	export function calculateEventPoints(
160	  _eventId: string,
161	  scores: EventScoreInput[],
162	  scheme: WorkoutScheme,
163	  config: ScoringConfig,
164	): Map<string, EventPointsResult> {
165	  if (scores.length === 0) {
166	    return new Map()
167	  }
168	
169	  switch (config.algorithm) {
170	    case "traditional":
171	      return calculateTraditionalEventPoints(scores, scheme, config)
172	    case "p_score":
173	      return calculatePScoreEventPoints(scores, scheme, config)
174	    case "winner_takes_more":
175	      return calculateWinnerTakesMoreEventPoints(scores, scheme, config)
176	    case "online":
177	      return calculateOnlineEventPoints(scores, scheme, config)
178	    case "custom":
179	      return calculateCustomEventPoints(scores, scheme, config)
180	    default: {
181	      // TypeScript exhaustiveness check
182	      const _exhaustive: never = config.algorithm
183	      throw new Error(`Unknown scoring algorithm: ${_exhaustive}`)
184	    }
185	  }
186	}
187	
188	/**
189	 * Calculate points using traditional (placement-based) scoring
190	 */
191	function calculateTraditionalEventPoints(
192	  scores: EventScoreInput[],
193	  scheme: WorkoutScheme,
194	  config: ScoringConfig,
195	): Map<string, EventPointsResult> {
196	  const traditionalConfig = config.traditional ?? DEFAULT_TRADITIONAL_CONFIG
197	
198	  // Separate active scores from inactive
199	  const activeScores = scores.filter(
200	    (s) => s.status === "scored" || s.status === "cap",
201	  )
202	  const inactiveScores = scores.filter(
203	    (s) => s.status === "dnf" || s.status === "dns" || s.status === "withdrawn",
204	  )
205	
206	  // Sort active scores by performance (uses sortKey when available)
207	  const sortedActive = sortScoresByPerformance(activeScores, scheme)
208	
209	  // Assign ranks with tie handling
210	  const ranked = assignRanks(sortedActive)
211	  const results = new Map<string, EventPointsResult>()
212	
213	  // Calculate points for active athletes
214	  for (const { score, rank } of ranked) {
215	    const points = calculateTraditionalPoints(rank, traditionalConfig)
216	    results.set(score.userId, {
217	      userId: score.userId,
218	      points,
219	      rank,
220	    })
221	  }
222	
223	  // Handle inactive athletes based on config
224	  const lastActiveRank = ranked.length > 0 ? ranked[ranked.length - 1].rank : 0
225	  for (const score of inactiveScores) {
226	    if (
227	      score.status !== "dnf" &&
228	      score.status !== "dns" &&
229	      score.status !== "withdrawn"
230	    ) {
231	      continue
232	    }
233	    const handling = getStatusHandling(score.status, config)
234	    let points: number
235	    let rank: number
236	
237	    switch (handling) {
238	      case "last_place":
239	      case "worst_performance":
240	        // Both get ranked after all active athletes
241	        rank = lastActiveRank + 1
242	        points = calculateTraditionalPoints(rank, traditionalConfig)
243	        break
244	      case "zero":
245	        rank = lastActiveRank + 1
246	        points = 0
247	        break
248	      case "exclude":
249	        // Don't add to results
250	        continue
251	    }
252	
253	    results.set(score.userId, {
254	      userId: score.userId,
255	      points,
256	      rank,
257	    })
258	  }
259	
260	  return results
261	}
262	
263	/**
264	 * Calculate points using Winner Takes More scoring
265	 * Uses a front-loaded points table that rewards top finishers more heavily
266	 */
267	function calculateWinnerTakesMoreEventPoints(
268	  scores: EventScoreInput[],
269	  scheme: WorkoutScheme,
270	  config: ScoringConfig,
271	): Map<string, EventPointsResult> {
272	  // Separate active scores from inactive
273	  const activeScores = scores.filter(
274	    (s) => s.status === "scored" || s.status === "cap",
275	  )
276	  const inactiveScores = scores.filter(
277	    (s) => s.status === "dnf" || s.status === "dns" || s.status === "withdrawn",
278	  )
279	
280	  // Sort active scores by performance (uses sortKey when available)
281	  const sortedActive = sortScoresByPerformance(activeScores, scheme)
282	
283	  // Assign ranks with tie handling
284	  const ranked = assignRanks(sortedActive)
285	  const results = new Map<string, EventPointsResult>()
286	
287	  // Calculate points for active athletes using winner_takes_more table
288	  for (const { score, rank } of ranked) {
289	    const points = calculateCustomPoints(rank, {
290	      baseTemplate: "winner_takes_more",
291	      overrides: {},
292	    })
293	    results.set(score.userId, {
294	      userId: score.userId,
295	      points,
296	      rank,
297	    })
298	  }
299	
300	  // Handle inactive athletes based on config
301	  const lastActiveRank = ranked.length > 0 ? ranked[ranked.length - 1].rank : 0
302	  for (const score of inactiveScores) {
303	    if (
304	      score.status !== "dnf" &&
305	      score.status !== "dns" &&
306	      score.status !== "withdrawn"
307	    ) {
308	      continue
309	    }
310	    const handling = getStatusHandling(score.status, config)
311	    let points: number
312	    let rank: number
313	
314	    switch (handling) {
315	      case "last_place":
316	      case "worst_performance":
317	        // Both get ranked after all active athletes
318	        rank = lastActiveRank + 1
319	        points = calculateCustomPoints(rank, {
320	          baseTemplate: "winner_takes_more",
321	          overrides: {},
322	        })
323	        break
324	      case "zero":
325	        rank = lastActiveRank + 1
326	        points = 0
327	        break
328	      case "exclude":
329	        // Don't add to results
330	        continue
331	    }
332	
333	    results.set(score.userId, {
334	      userId: score.userId,
335	      points,
336	      rank,
337	    })
338	  }
339	
340	  return results
341	}
342	
343	/**
344	 * Calculate points using P-Score (performance-based) scoring
345	 */
346	/**
347	 * Map WorkoutScheme to P-Score compatible scheme
348	 */
349	function mapToPScoreScheme(scheme: WorkoutScheme): PScoreInput["scheme"] {
350	  switch (scheme) {
351	    case "time":
352	      return "time"
353	    case "time-with-cap":
354	      return "time-with-cap"
355	    case "reps":
356	    case "rounds-reps":
357	    case "calories":
358	      return "reps"
359	    case "load":
360	      return "load"
361	    case "points":
362	    case "pass-fail":
363	    case "emom":
364	    case "meters":
365	    case "feet":
366	      return "points"
367	  }
368	}
369	
370	function calculatePScoreEventPoints(
371	  scores: EventScoreInput[],
372	  scheme: WorkoutScheme,
373	  config: ScoringConfig,
374	): Map<string, EventPointsResult> {
375	  const pScoreConfig = config.pScore ?? DEFAULT_PSCORE_CONFIG
376	  const pScoreScheme = mapToPScoreScheme(scheme)
377	
378	  const input: PScoreInput = {
379	    scores: scores.map((s) => ({
380	      userId: s.userId,
381	      value: s.value,
382	      status: s.status,
383	    })),
384	    scheme: pScoreScheme,
385	    config: pScoreConfig,
386	  }
387	
388	  const pScoreResults = calculatePScore(input)
389	  const results = new Map<string, EventPointsResult>()
390	
391	  for (const result of pScoreResults) {
392	    results.set(result.userId, {
393	      userId: result.userId,
394	      points: result.pScore,
395	      rank: result.rank,
396	    })
397	  }
398	
399	  return results
400	}
401	
402	/**
403	 * Calculate points using custom table scoring
404	 */
405	function calculateCustomEventPoints(
406	  scores: EventScoreInput[],
407	  scheme: WorkoutScheme,
408	  config: ScoringConfig,
409	): Map<string, EventPointsResult> {
410	  const customConfig = config.customTable ?? {
411	    baseTemplate: "traditional" as const,
412	    overrides: {},
413	  }
414	  const traditionalConfig = config.traditional ?? DEFAULT_TRADITIONAL_CONFIG
415	
416	  // Separate active scores from inactive
417	  const activeScores = scores.filter(
418	    (s) => s.status === "scored" || s.status === "cap",
419	  )
420	  const inactiveScores = scores.filter(
421	    (s) => s.status === "dnf" || s.status === "dns" || s.status === "withdrawn",
422	  )
423	
424	  // Sort active scores by performance (uses sortKey when available)
425	  const sortedActive = sortScoresByPerformance(activeScores, scheme)
426	
427	  // Assign ranks with tie handling
428	  const ranked = assignRanks(sortedActive)
429	  const results = new Map<string, EventPointsResult>()
430	
431	  // Calculate points for active athletes
432	  for (const { score, rank } of ranked) {
433	    const points = calculateCustomPoints(rank, customConfig, traditionalConfig)
434	    results.set(score.userId, {
435	      userId: score.userId,
436	      points,
437	      rank,
438	    })
439	  }
440	
441	  // Handle inactive athletes based on config
442	  const lastActiveRank = ranked.length > 0 ? ranked[ranked.length - 1].rank : 0
443	  for (const score of inactiveScores) {
444	    if (
445	      score.status !== "dnf" &&
446	      score.status !== "dns" &&
447	      score.status !== "withdrawn"
448	    ) {
449	      continue
450	    }
451	    const handling = getStatusHandling(score.status, config)
452	    let points: number
453	    let rank: number
454	
455	    switch (handling) {
456	      case "last_place":
457	      case "worst_performance":
458	        rank = lastActiveRank + 1
459	        points = calculateCustomPoints(rank, customConfig, traditionalConfig)
460	        break
461	      case "zero":
462	        rank = lastActiveRank + 1
463	        points = 0
464	        break
465	      case "exclude":
466	        continue
467	    }
468	
469	    results.set(score.userId, {
470	      userId: score.userId,
471	      points,
472	      rank,
473	    })
474	  }
475	
476	  return results
477	}
478	
479	/**
480	 * Calculate points using online scoring
481	 * Points = rank (1st place = 1 point, 2nd = 2 points, etc.)
482	 * Lowest total wins, ideal for online competitions with unknown participant count
483	 */
484	function calculateOnlineEventPoints(
485	  scores: EventScoreInput[],
486	  scheme: WorkoutScheme,
487	  config: ScoringConfig,
488	): Map<string, EventPointsResult> {
489	  // Separate active scores from inactive
490	  const activeScores = scores.filter(
491	    (s) => s.status === "scored" || s.status === "cap",
492	  )
493	  const inactiveScores = scores.filter(
494	    (s) => s.status === "dnf" || s.status === "dns" || s.status === "withdrawn",
495	  )
496	
497	  // Sort active scores by performance (uses sortKey when available)
498	  const sortedActive = sortScoresByPerformance(activeScores, scheme)
499	
500	  // Assign ranks with tie handling
501	  const ranked = assignRanks(sortedActive)
502	  const results = new Map<string, EventPointsResult>()
503	
504	  // Calculate points for active athletes (points = rank)
505	  for (const { score, rank } of ranked) {
506	    const points = calculateOnlinePoints(rank)
507	    results.set(score.userId, {
508	      userId: score.userId,
509	      points,
510	      rank,
511	    })
512	  }
513	
514	  // Handle inactive athletes based on config
515	  const lastActiveRank = ranked.length > 0 ? ranked[ranked.length - 1].rank : 0
516	  for (const score of inactiveScores) {
517	    if (
518	      score.status !== "dnf" &&
519	      score.status !== "dns" &&
520	      score.status !== "withdrawn"
521	    ) {
522	      continue
523	    }
524	    const handling = getStatusHandling(score.status, config)
525	    let points: number
526	    let rank: number
527	
528	    switch (handling) {
529	      case "last_place":
530	      case "worst_performance":
531	        // Both get ranked after all active athletes
532	        rank = lastActiveRank + 1
533	        points = calculateOnlinePoints(rank)
534	        break
535	      case "zero":
536	        // For online scoring, "zero" means worst possible position
537	        // Rank and points should stay consistent (points = rank)
538	        // Use total participants + 1 as the penalty position
539	        rank = scores.length + 1
540	        points = calculateOnlinePoints(rank)
541	        break
542	      case "exclude":
543	        // Don't add to results
544	        continue
545	    }
546	
547	    results.set(score.userId, {
548	      userId: score.userId,
549	      points,
550	      rank,
551	    })
552	  }
553	
554	  return results
555	}
556	
557	/**
558	 * Assign ranks to sorted scores, handling ties.
559	 * Uses sortKey for tie detection when available, falls back to value.
560	 */
561	function assignRanks(
562	  sortedScores: EventScoreInput[],
563	): Array<{ score: EventScoreInput; rank: number }> {
564	  const result: Array<{ score: EventScoreInput; rank: number }> = []
565	  let currentRank = 1
566	  let previousSortKey: string | null = null
567	  let previousValue: number | null = null
568	
569	  for (let i = 0; i < sortedScores.length; i++) {
570	    const score = sortedScores[i]
571	
572	    // Use sortKey for tie detection when available (more accurate)
573	    if (score.sortKey && previousSortKey !== null) {
574	      if (score.sortKey !== previousSortKey) {
575	        currentRank = i + 1
576	      }
577	    } else if (previousValue !== null && score.value !== previousValue) {
578	      // Fallback to value-only comparison
579	      currentRank = i + 1
580	    }
581	
582	    result.push({ score, rank: currentRank })
583	    previousSortKey = score.sortKey ?? null
584	    previousValue = score.value
585	  }
586	
587	  return result
588	}
589	
590	/**
591	 * Status handling result for internal use
592	 */
593	type StatusHandlingResult =
594	  | "last_place"
595	  | "worst_performance"
596	  | "zero"
597	  | "exclude"
598	
599	/**
600	 * Get handling mode for a given status
601	 */
602	function getStatusHandling(
603	  status: "dnf" | "dns" | "withdrawn",
604	  config: ScoringConfig,
605	): StatusHandlingResult {
606	  const handling =
607	    config.statusHandling ?? DEFAULT_SCORING_CONFIG.statusHandling
608	
609	  switch (status) {
610	    case "dnf":
611	      return handling.dnf ?? "last_place"
612	    case "dns":
613	      return handling.dns ?? "zero"
614	    case "withdrawn":
615	      return handling.withdrawn ?? "exclude"
616	  }
617	}
618	
619	/**
620	 * Calculate the points awarded for a specific finishing place under the
621	 * configured scoring algorithm. Used to award missing-score athletes the
622	 * "tied for worst" position without running them through the full scoring
623	 * pipeline.
624	 *
625	 * P-Score returns 0 because P-Score has no static place→points mapping;
626	 * inactive athletes always get 0 (matching `calculatePScore` behavior).
627	 */
628	export function calculatePointsForPlace({
629	  place,
630	  config,
631	}: {
632	  place: number
633	  config: ScoringConfig
634	}): number {
635	  switch (config.algorithm) {
636	    case "traditional":
637	      return calculateTraditionalPoints(
638	        place,
639	        config.traditional ?? DEFAULT_TRADITIONAL_CONFIG,
640	      )
641	    case "online":
642	      return calculateOnlinePoints(place)
643	    case "winner_takes_more":
644	      return calculateCustomPoints(place, {
645	        baseTemplate: "winner_takes_more",
646	        overrides: {},
647	      })
648	    case "custom":
649	      return calculateCustomPoints(
650	        place,
651	        config.customTable ?? {
652	          baseTemplate: "traditional" as const,
653	          overrides: {},
654	        },
655	        config.traditional ?? DEFAULT_TRADITIONAL_CONFIG,
656	      )
657	    case "p_score":
658	      return 0
659	    default: {
660	      const _exhaustive: never = config.algorithm
661	      throw new Error(`Unknown scoring algorithm: ${_exhaustive}`)
662	    }
663	  }
664	}
665	
666	/**
667	 * Get scoring algorithm display name
668	 */
669	export function getScoringAlgorithmName(
670	  algorithm: ScoringConfig["algorithm"],
671	): string {
672	  switch (algorithm) {
673	    case "traditional":
674	      return "Traditional"
675	    case "p_score":
676	      return "P-Score"
677	    case "winner_takes_more":
678	      return "Winner Takes More"
679	    case "online":
680	      return "Online"
681	    case "custom":
682	      return "Custom"
683	  }
684	}
685	
686	/**
687	 * Check if scoring algorithm supports negative scores
688	 */
689	export function canHaveNegativeScores(config: ScoringConfig): boolean {
690	  return (
691	    config.algorithm === "p_score" && (config.pScore?.allowNegatives ?? true)
692	  )
693	}

content.content:
1	/**
2	 * Competition Leaderboard Server Logic
3	 *
4	 * Provides leaderboard calculation for competitions using the configurable
5	 * scoring system. Supports Traditional, P-Score, and Custom scoring algorithms
6	 * with configurable tiebreakers.
7	 *
8	 * @see docs/plans/configurable-scoring-system.md
9	 * @see @/lib/scoring/algorithms - Scoring algorithm implementations
10	 * @see @/lib/scoring/tiebreakers - Tiebreaker logic
11	 */
12	
13	import { and, eq, inArray, isNull, ne, or } from "drizzle-orm"
14	import { getDb } from "@/db"
15	import { logInfo, logWarning } from "@/lib/logging"
16	import {
17	  competitionHeatAssignmentsTable,
18	  competitionHeatsTable,
19	  competitionRegistrationsTable,
20	  competitionsTable,
21	  REGISTRATION_STATUS,
22	} from "@/db/schemas/competitions"
23	import { eventDivisionMappingsTable } from "@/db/schemas/event-division-mappings"
24	import {
25	  programmingTracksTable,
26	  trackWorkoutsTable,
27	} from "@/db/schemas/programming"
28	import { scalingLevelsTable } from "@/db/schemas/scaling"
29	import { scoreRoundsTable, scoresTable } from "@/db/schemas/scores"
30	import { teamMembershipTable } from "@/db/schemas/teams"
31	import { userTable } from "@/db/schemas/users"
32	import {
33	  type ReviewStatus,
34	  videoSubmissionsTable,
35	} from "@/db/schemas/video-submissions"
36	import { workouts } from "@/db/schemas/workouts"
37	import {
38	  calculateEventPoints,
39	  calculatePointsForPlace,
40	  computeSortKey,
41	  DEFAULT_SCORING_CONFIG,
42	  decodeScore,
43	  type EventScoreInput,
44	  formatScore,
45	  getDefaultScoreType,
46	  isTimeBasedScheme,
47	  sortKeyToString,
48	  type WorkoutScheme,
49	} from "@/lib/scoring"
50	import {
51	  applyTiebreakers,
52	  type TiebreakerInput,
53	} from "@/lib/scoring/tiebreakers"
54	import {
55	  getEffectiveScoringConfig,
56	  parseCompetitionSettings,
57	} from "@/types/competitions"
58	import { getAffiliate } from "@/utils/registration-metadata"
59	
60	// ============================================================================
61	// Types
62	// ============================================================================
63	
64	export interface TeamMemberInfo {
65	  userId: string
66	  firstName: string | null
67	  lastName: string | null
68	  isCaptain: boolean
69	}
70	
71	export interface CompetitionLeaderboardEntry {
72	  registrationId: string
73	  userId: string
74	  athleteName: string
75	  divisionId: string
76	  divisionLabel: string
77	  totalPoints: number
78	  overallRank: number
79	  // Team info (null for individual divisions)
80	  isTeamDivision: boolean
81	  teamName: string | null
82	  teamMembers: TeamMemberInfo[]
83	  /** Affiliate/gym name from registration metadata */
84	  affiliate: string | null
85	  eventResults: Array<{
86	    trackWorkoutId: string
87	    trackOrder: number
88	    eventName: string
89	    scheme: string
90	    rank: number
91	    points: number
92	    rawScore: string | null
93	    formattedScore: string
94	    /** Formatted tiebreak value if present */
95	    formattedTiebreak: string | null
96	    /** Penalty info if score was adjusted */
97	    penaltyType: "minor" | "major" | null
98	    penaltyPercentage: number | null
99	    /** Whether score was directly modified (adjusted without penalty) */
100	    isDirectlyModified: boolean
101	    /** Video submission URL (online competitions only) */
102	    videoUrl: string | null
103	    /** Video submission ID for voting (online competitions only) */
104	    videoSubmissionId: string | null
105	    /** Parent event ID if this is a sub-event, null for standalone/parent */
106	    parentEventId: string | null
107	    /** Parent event name if this is a sub-event, null for standalone */
108	    parentEventName: string | null
109	    /** True if this is a parent event with aggregated sub-event points */
110	    isParentEvent: boolean
111	    /** Number of individual rounds marked as capped (0 for single-round or none) */
112	    cappedRoundCount: number
113	    /** Total number of rounds persisted for this score (0 for single-round) */
114	    totalRoundCount: number
115	    /**
116	     * Aggregate review status across all video submissions for this event.
117	     * Partner/team divisions can have multiple videos (one per teammate);
118	     * the summary collapses them so the leaderboard cell can render a single
119	     * indicator. `null` when the registration has no submission rows for this
120	     * event yet.
121	     */
122	    reviewSummary: EventReviewSummary | null
123	  }>
124	}
125	
126	/**
127	 * Aggregate of every video submission a registration has uploaded for a
128	 * single event. Powers the per-cell review indicator on the organizer
129	 * leaderboard preview — individual divisions resolve to one video, partner
130	 * divisions to N (one per teammate).
131	 */
132	export interface EventReviewSummary {
133	  /** Number of video rows that exist for this (registration, event). */
134	  totalSubmitted: number
135	  /** Expected video count = division `teamSize` (1 for individual). */
136	  expectedVideos: number
137	  /**
138	   * Count of submissions in a terminal review state — anything other than
139	   * `pending` or `under_review`. An organizer reading the leaderboard can
140	   * scan this against `totalSubmitted` to see what's still outstanding.
141	   */
142	  reviewedCount: number
143	  /** Distinct review statuses present across submissions, deduped. */
144	  statuses: ReviewStatus[]
145	  /**
146	   * The single status to surface when collapsing to one badge — picks the
147	   * highest-priority status across all submissions (see `REVIEW_STATUS_PRIORITY`).
148	   * Bias toward statuses that need organizer attention (pending > verified).
149	   */
150	  worstStatus: ReviewStatus
151	}
152	
153	/** Round-cap summary for a single score */
154	interface RoundCapSummary {
155	  cappedRoundCount: number
156	  totalRoundCount: number
157	}
158	
159	/**
160	 * Priority ordering used to collapse multiple review statuses into a single
161	 * "worst" status for the leaderboard cell indicator. Higher number = more
162	 * organizer attention required, so it wins. `pending` outranks any reviewed
163	 * status because an unreviewed video on a partner team is the actionable
164	 * state the organizer cares about even if the captain's video is verified.
165	 */
166	const REVIEW_STATUS_PRIORITY: Record<ReviewStatus, number> = {
167	  pending: 6,
168	  under_review: 5,
169	  invalid: 4,
170	  penalized: 3,
171	  adjusted: 2,
172	  verified: 1,
173	}
174	
175	export interface EventLeaderboardEntry {
176	  registrationId: string
177	  userId: string
178	  athleteName: string
179	  divisionId: string
180	  divisionLabel: string
181	  rank: number
182	  points: number
183	  rawScore: string | null
184	  formattedScore: string
185	  isTimeCapped: boolean
186	}
187	
188	export interface CompetitionLeaderboardResult {
189	  entries: CompetitionLeaderboardEntry[]
190	  scoringConfig: import("@/types/scoring").ScoringConfig
191	  events: Array<{
192	    trackWorkoutId: string
193	    name: string
194	    parentEventId: string | null
195	    parentEventName: string | null
196	    isParentEvent: boolean
197	  }>
198	}
199	
200	// ============================================================================
201	// Helper Functions
202	// ============================================================================
203	
204	/**
205	 * Fetch scores from the scores table.
206	 *
207	 * In preview mode, we return every score regardless of `verificationStatus`
208	 * (including `"invalid"`) so organizers can see the full raw picture. The
209	 * verification status comes back on each entry so the UI can flag it.
210	 * The public path still excludes invalidated scores.
211	 */
212	async function fetchScores(params: {
213	  trackWorkoutIds: string[]
214	  userIds: string[]
215	  includeInvalid?: boolean
216	}) {
217	  const db = getDb()
218	
219	  const scores = await db
220	    .select({
221	      id: scoresTable.id,
222	      userId: scoresTable.userId,
223	      competitionEventId: scoresTable.competitionEventId,
224	      scheme: scoresTable.scheme,
225	      scoreValue: scoresTable.scoreValue,
226	      tiebreakScheme: scoresTable.tiebreakScheme,
227	      tiebreakValue: scoresTable.tiebreakValue,
228	      status: scoresTable.status,
229	      statusOrder: scoresTable.statusOrder,
230	      sortKey: scoresTable.sortKey,
231	      secondaryValue: scoresTable.secondaryValue,
232	      timeCapMs: scoresTable.timeCapMs,
233	      verificationStatus: scoresTable.verificationStatus,
234	      penaltyType: scoresTable.penaltyType,
235	      penaltyPercentage: scoresTable.penaltyPercentage,
236	    })
237	    .from(scoresTable)
238	    .where(
239	      params.includeInvalid
240	        ? and(
241	            inArray(scoresTable.competitionEventId, params.trackWorkoutIds),
242	            inArray(scoresTable.userId, params.userIds),
243	          )
244	        : and(
245	            inArray(scoresTable.competitionEventId, params.trackWorkoutIds),
246	            inArray(scoresTable.userId, params.userIds),
247	            // Exclude invalidated scores from leaderboard
248	            or(
249	              isNull(scoresTable.verificationStatus),
250	              ne(scoresTable.verificationStatus, "invalid"),
251	            ),
252	          ),
253	    )
254	
255	  return scores
256	}
257	
258	/**
259	 * For each score id, count how many rounds were persisted and how many
260	 * of those carry `status = "cap"`. Drives two things at once:
261	 *
262	 * 1. The capped-rounds badge on multi-round leaderboard scores (without
263	 *    shipping the full round payload to the client).
264	 * 2. The in-flight `computeSortKey` recomputation below, which uses
265	 *    `cappedRoundCount` as a dominant tiebreaker inside the cap bucket
266	 *    ("fewer caps beats more caps").
267	 */
268	async function fetchRoundCapSummaries(
269	  scoreIds: string[],
270	): Promise<Map<string, RoundCapSummary>> {
271	  const summaries = new Map<string, RoundCapSummary>()
272	  if (scoreIds.length === 0) return summaries
273	
274	  const db = getDb()
275	  const rows = await db
276	    .select({
277	      scoreId: scoreRoundsTable.scoreId,
278	      status: scoreRoundsTable.status,
279	    })
280	    .from(scoreRoundsTable)
281	    .where(inArray(scoreRoundsTable.scoreId, scoreIds))
282	
283	  for (const row of rows) {
284	    const existing = summaries.get(row.scoreId) ?? {
285	      cappedRoundCount: 0,
286	      totalRoundCount: 0,
287	    }
288	    existing.totalRoundCount += 1
289	    if (row.status === "cap") existing.cappedRoundCount += 1
290	    summaries.set(row.scoreId, existing)
291	  }
292	
293	  return summaries
294	}
295	
296	/**
297	 * Map score status to EventScoreInput status
298	 */
299	function mapScoreStatus(status: string | null): EventScoreInput["status"] {
300	  switch (status) {
301	    case "scored":
302	      return "scored"
303	    case "cap":
304	      return "cap"
305	    case "dq":
306	    case "dnf":
307	      return "dnf"
308	    case "withdrawn":
309	      return "withdrawn"
310	    default:
311	      return "dns"
312	  }
313	}
314	
315	/**
316	 * Get competition track for a competition
317	 */
318	async function getCompetitionTrack(competitionId: string) {
319	  const db = getDb()
320	  return db.query.programmingTracksTable.findFirst({
321	    where: eq(programmingTracksTable.competitionId, competitionId),
322	  })
323	}
324	
325	// ============================================================================
326	// Heat-Based Workout Filtering
327	// ============================================================================
328	
329	interface HeatInfo {
330	  id: string
331	  trackWorkoutId: string
332	  divisionId: string | null
333	}
334	
335	interface HeatAssignmentInfo {
336	  heatId: string
337	  divisionId: string | null
338	}
339	
340	/**
341	 * Determine which track workouts are relevant for a division based on heat data.
342	 *
343	 * A workout is relevant to a division if:
344	 * 1. A heat exists with divisionId matching the target division, OR
345	 * 2. A mixed heat (divisionId=null) has at least one assignment from that division
346	 *
347	 * Returns null if no heats exist (backward compat: show all workouts).
348	 */
349	export function getRelevantWorkoutIds(params: {
350	  heats: HeatInfo[]
351	  mixedHeatAssignments: HeatAssignmentInfo[]
352	  divisionId: string
353	}): Set<string> | null {
354	  if (params.heats.length === 0) return null
355	
356	  // Workouts with division-specific heats matching selected division
357	  const relevant = new Set(
358	    params.heats
359	      .filter((h) => h.divisionId === params.divisionId)
360	      .map((h) => h.trackWorkoutId),
361	  )
362	
363	  // For mixed heats (divisionId=null), check actual assignments
364	  const heatIdToWorkout = new Map(
365	    params.heats
366	      .filter((h) => h.divisionId === null)
367	      .map((h) => [h.id, h.trackWorkoutId]),
368	  )
369	
370	  for (const assignment of params.mixedHeatAssignments) {
371	    if (assignment.divisionId === params.divisionId) {
372	      const twId = heatIdToWorkout.get(assignment.heatId)
373	      if (twId) relevant.add(twId)
374	    }
375	  }
376	
377	  return relevant
378	}
379	
380	// ============================================================================
381	// Main Leaderboard Functions
382	// ============================================================================
383	
384	/**
385	 * Get the competition leaderboard with configurable scoring.
386	 *
387	 * Uses the ScoringConfig from competition settings to determine:
388	 * - Scoring algorithm (traditional, p_score, custom)
389	 * - Tiebreaker method (countback, head_to_head, none)
390	 * - Status handling (DNF, DNS, withdrawn)
391	 */
392	export async function getCompetitionLeaderboard(params: {
393	  competitionId: string
394	  divisionId?: string
395	  /**
396	   * When true, skip both the event-visibility (`eventStatus`) filter and
397	   * the per-division `divisionResults` publishing filter — include every
398	   * track workout and score regardless of publish state. Used by the
399	   * organizer leaderboard preview so organizers can see aggregated
400	   * standings before publishing events to athletes. Must be authorized at
401	   * the caller layer — this function does not enforce organizer permissions.
402	   */
403	  bypassPublicationFilter?: boolean
404	}): Promise<CompetitionLeaderboardResult> {
405	  const db = getDb()
406	
407	  logInfo({
408	    message: "[Leaderboard] getCompetitionLeaderboard start",
409	    attributes: {
410	      competitionId: params.competitionId,
411	      divisionId: params.divisionId ?? null,
412	      bypassPublicationFilter: params.bypassPublicationFilter === true,
413	    },
414	  })
415	
416	  // Get competition with settings
417	  const competition = await db.query.competitionsTable.findFirst({
418	    where: eq(competitionsTable.id, params.competitionId),
419	  })
420	
421	  if (!competition) {
422	    logWarning({
423	      message: "[Leaderboard] Competition not found",
424	      attributes: { competitionId: params.competitionId },
425	    })
426	    throw new Error("Competition not found")
427	  }
428	
429	  // Parse settings and get scoring config
430	  const settings = parseCompetitionSettings(competition.settings)
431	  const scoringConfig =
432	    getEffectiveScoringConfig(settings) ?? DEFAULT_SCORING_CONFIG
433	
434	  // Division results publishing state — controls leaderboard visibility.
435	  // For online competitions, default to empty (everything hidden until explicitly published).
436	  // For in-person competitions, absent divisionResults means show all (backwards compat).
437	  // When `bypassPublicationFilter` is set, we treat divisionResults as undefined so
438	  // every score is included (used by the organizer preview).
439	  const divisionResults = params.bypassPublicationFilter
440	    ? undefined
441	    : (settings?.divisionResults ??
442	      (competition.competitionType === "online" ? {} : undefined))
443	
444	  logInfo({
445	    message: "[Leaderboard] Publication gates",
446	    attributes: {
447	      competitionId: params.competitionId,
448	      bypassPublicationFilter: params.bypassPublicationFilter === true,
449	      competitionType: competition.competitionType,
450	      hasDivisionResultsSetting: !!settings?.divisionResults,
451	      divisionResultsActive: !!divisionResults,
452	    },
453	  })
454	
455	  // Get competition track
456	  const track = await getCompetitionTrack(params.competitionId)
457	  if (!track) {
458	    logWarning({
459	      message: "[Leaderboard] No programming track for competition",
460	      attributes: { competitionId: params.competitionId },
461	    })
462	    return { entries: [], scoringConfig, events: [] }
463	  }
464	
465	  // Get all track workouts for this competition.
466	  // In preview mode, include draft events too — organizers can enter scores
467	  // on draft events and need to see them before publishing.
468	  const trackWorkouts = await db
469	    .select({
470	      id: trackWorkoutsTable.id,
471	      trackOrder: trackWorkoutsTable.trackOrder,
472	      pointsMultiplier: trackWorkoutsTable.pointsMultiplier,
473	      workoutId: trackWorkoutsTable.workoutId,
474	      parentEventId: trackWorkoutsTable.parentEventId,
475	      eventStatus: trackWorkoutsTable.eventStatus,
476	      workout: workouts,
477	    })
478	    .from(trackWorkoutsTable)
479	    .innerJoin(workouts, eq(trackWorkoutsTable.workoutId, workouts.id))
480	    .where(
481	      params.bypassPublicationFilter
482	        ? eq(trackWorkoutsTable.trackId, track.id)
483	        : and(
484	            eq(trackWorkoutsTable.trackId, track.id),
485	            eq(trackWorkoutsTable.eventStatus, "published"),
486	          ),
487	    )
488	    .orderBy(trackWorkoutsTable.trackOrder)
489	
490	  const draftTrackWorkouts = trackWorkouts.filter(
491	    (tw) => tw.eventStatus !== "published",
492	  ).length
493	
494	  logInfo({
495	    message: "[Leaderboard] Track workouts loaded",
496	    attributes: {
497	      competitionId: params.competitionId,
498	      trackId: track.id,
499	      trackWorkoutCount: trackWorkouts.length,
500	      // Preview includes draft events — count separately so we know if this
501	      // pathway is pulling in draft events that the public query would skip
502	      draftEventsIncluded: params.bypassPublicationFilter
503	        ? draftTrackWorkouts
504	        : 0,
505	    },
506	  })
507	
508	  if (trackWorkouts.length === 0) {
509	    logWarning({
510	      message: "[Leaderboard] No track workouts match filters",
511	      attributes: {
512	        competitionId: params.competitionId,
513	        trackId: track.id,
514	        bypassPublicationFilter: params.bypassPublicationFilter === true,
515	      },
516	    })
517	    return { entries: [], scoringConfig, events: [] }
518	  }
519	
520	  // Filter workouts by division when a division is selected.
521	  // Two filtering mechanisms (event-division mappings take priority over heats):
522	  // 1. Event-division mappings: explicit organizer-configured event↔division assignments
523	  // 2. Heat-based filtering: fallback using heat assignments when no explicit mappings exist
524	  let filteredTrackWorkouts = trackWorkouts
525	  if (params.divisionId) {
526	    // Check for explicit event-division mappings first
527	    let eventDivisionMappings: Array<{ trackWorkoutId: string }> = []
528	    try {
529	      eventDivisionMappings = await db
530	        .select({
531	          trackWorkoutId: eventDivisionMappingsTable.trackWorkoutId,
532	        })
533	        .from(eventDivisionMappingsTable)
534	        .where(
535	          eq(eventDivisionMappingsTable.competitionId, params.competitionId),
536	        )
537	    } catch (error: unknown) {
538	      if (
539	        error &&
540	        typeof error === "object" &&
541	        "code" in error &&
542	        (error as { code?: string | number }).code === "ER_NO_SUCH_TABLE"
543	      ) {
544	        // Table may not exist yet — skip mapping-based filtering
545	      } else {
546	        throw error
547	      }
548	    }
549	
550	    if (eventDivisionMappings.length > 0) {
551	      // Mappings exist — filter to events mapped to this division
552	      const mappedToDiv = await db
553	        .select({
554	          trackWorkoutId: eventDivisionMappingsTable.trackWorkoutId,
555	        })
556	        .from(eventDivisionMappingsTable)
557	        .where(
558	          and(
559	            eq(eventDivisionMappingsTable.competitionId, params.competitionId),
560	            eq(eventDivisionMappingsTable.divisionId, params.divisionId),
561	          ),
562	        )
563	
564	      const mappedToSelectedDiv = new Set(
565	        mappedToDiv.map((m) => m.trackWorkoutId),
566	      )
567	      const allMappedEventIds = new Set(
568	        eventDivisionMappings.map((m) => m.trackWorkoutId),
569	      )
570	
571	      // Only filter events that have explicit mappings; unmapped events stay visible
572	      filteredTrackWorkouts = trackWorkouts.filter((tw) => {
573	        const hasMapping =
574	          allMappedEventIds.has(tw.id) ||
575	          (tw.parentEventId && allMappedEventIds.has(tw.parentEventId))
576	        if (!hasMapping) return true // unmapped → visible to all
577	        return (
578	          mappedToSelectedDiv.has(tw.id) ||
579	          (tw.parentEventId && mappedToSelectedDiv.has(tw.parentEventId))
580	        )
581	      })
582	    } else {
583	      // No explicit mappings — fall back to heat-based filtering
584	      const trackWorkoutIds = trackWorkouts.map((tw) => tw.id)
585	      const heatsForWorkouts = await db
586	        .select({
587	          id: competitionHeatsTable.id,
588	          trackWorkoutId: competitionHeatsTable.trackWorkoutId,
589	          divisionId: competitionHeatsTable.divisionId,
590	        })
591	        .from(competitionHeatsTable)
592	        .where(inArray(competitionHeatsTable.trackWorkoutId, trackWorkoutIds))
593	
594	      // Fetch assignments for mixed heats (divisionId=null)
595	      const mixedHeatIds = heatsForWorkouts
